Patch Set 4: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(25 inline comments)

The naming of functions could be more consistent:

 mdt_hsm_coordinator_get_running
 mdt_hsm_restore_is_running
 mdt_hsm_coordinator_get_actions
 mdt_cdt_check_action_compat
 mdt_coordinator_wakeup
 mdt_agent_find_compatible_cb
 mdt_agent_find_compatible
 mdt_check_action_needed
 mdt_hsm_coordinator_actions

Could we use a more consistent (and concise) prefix, like mdt_hsm_get_running() or similar.

Patch Set 4: (20 inline comments)

for the naming, I will use mdt_hsm_XXX for public functions and hsm_XXX for internal.

Patch Set 4:

Issue to move hal_is_sane to lustre_user.h. fid_is_sane is needed but declared inline in lustre_idl.h. lustre_idl.h include lustre_user.h, how to solve this ?

Patch Set 4: (1 inline comment)

> Issue to move hal_is_sane to lustre_user.h. fid_is_sane is needed but declared inline in lustre_idl.h. lustre_idl.h include lustre_user.h, how to solve this ?

I guess we don't want fid_is_sane() in userspace. Never mind.

Patch Set 11:

JC, I am trying to cherry pick the HSM changes onto master but am seeing some conflicts.Is there a preferred order for the three series? Based on the landing priorities Jodi assumes, the order is release, coordinator, then copytool. Is that correct?

The changes here to sanity-hsm.sh wil need rebasing if the release series goes first.

Also note that the changes to sanity-hsm.sh at the end of the coordinator series introduce dependence on the copytool.

parents.subject: LU-3611 quota: Fix order of locking and allocating memory.

commit.subject: LU-3341 mdt: HSM coordinator client interface
commit.message:
LU-3341 mdt: HSM coordinator client interface

This patch implements the HSM coordinator methods
used by client to add/remove/list HSM actions on
FID.

Signed-off-by: JC Lafoucriere <jacques-charles.lafoucriere@cea.fr>
Change-Id: I0f2d7bfc3d98d98cb13040fad0f7f585c6f3dadf
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/6532
Tested-by: Hudson
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
